title: STRENDS - Argument compatibility rules are not followed
description:
Query 1:
SELECT ?S WHERE
{
    BIND("test string"@cz AS ?S)
    FILTER(STRENDS(?S,"ing"@en))
}

Query 2:
SELECT ?S WHERE
{
    BIND("test string" AS ?S)
    FILTER(STRENDS(?S,"ing"@en))
}

Result 1:
"test string"@cz

Result 2:
"test string"

But according to SPARQL 1.1 documentation (http://www.w3.org/TR/sparql11-query/#func-arg-compatibility) the results should be empty, because this usage of STREDNS should raise an error (a language tag of the second argument must be same as a language tag of the first argument).
key_bug_features: STRENDS function ignores language tag compatibility rules
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, lang_tag VARCHAR(10));
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, value, lang_tag) VALUES (1, 'test string', 'cz');
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, value, lang_tag) VALUES (2, 'test string', NULL);
SELECT value FROM test_strings WHERE lang_tag IS NOT NULL AND STRENDS(value, 'ing'::VARCHAR);
SELECT value FROM test_strings WHERE lang_tag = 'en' AND STRENDS(value, 'ing'::VARCHAR);
